{"id":"flotsync-vrt.4","title":"Add protobuf schema and UpdateId wire access for datamodel transport","description":"Define protobuf messages for the schema snapshot and schema operation surface specialized to UpdateId, and add the minimal flotsync_core API needed for flotsync_messages to construct and inspect UpdateId during wire conversion.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf messages cover dataset snapshots, row snapshots, history node streams, state field payloads, and schema operations using UpdateId. 2) flotsync_core exposes the minimal public API needed to encode and decode UpdateId outside the crate. 3) The message layout is documented well enough for the codec tasks to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","labels":["area:messages-protobuf"]}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.4","title":"Add protobuf schema and UpdateId wire access for datamodel transport","description":"Define protobuf messages for the schema snapshot and schema operation surface specialized to UpdateId, and add the minimal flotsync_core API needed for flotsync_messages to construct and inspect UpdateId during wire conversion.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf messages cover dataset snapshots, row snapshots, history node streams, state field payloads, and schema operations using UpdateId. 2) flotsync_core exposes the minimal public API needed to encode and decode UpdateId outside the crate. 3) The message layout is documented well enough for the codec tasks to implement against it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T18:21:46Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T18:21:46Z","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["area:messages-protobuf"]}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.3","title":"Implement protobuf snapshot visitors and dataset decoders","description":"Implement SchemaSnapshotEncoder, SchemaSnapshotDecoder, DataSnapshotEncoder, and DataSnapshotDecoder in flotsync_messages for the specialized protobuf format, following the existing schema/datamodel snapshot flow end to end.","acceptance_criteria":"1) flotsync_messages provides working protobuf-backed implementations of the schema snapshot visitor and decoder traits. 2) Dataset-level row encoding and lazy row decoding work through InMemoryData encode_data_snapshots and decode_data_snapshots. 3) The implementation uses the specialized snapshot payload codecs rather than per-CRDT bespoke paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:29Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.5","title":"Implement direct protobuf conversions for schema operations","description":"Implement direct protobuf message conversions for SchemaOperation\u003cUpdateId\u003e and OperationValue\u003cUpdateId\u003e across the full datamodel operation surface, including history-backed and state-based field variants.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Protobuf conversions cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, LinearList, MonotonicCounter, TotalOrderRegister, and TotalOrderFiniteStateRegister operation variants. 2) LinearString and LinearList insert and delete forms are both represented. 3) The helpers validate or preserve enough structure to roundtrip back into the datamodel operation APIs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:30Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-02T17:41:30Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6","title":"Benchmark a columnar HistorySnapshot format against the current node-oriented encoding","description":"Once the baseline protobuf snapshot encoder/decoder exists, add an alternative HistorySnapshot storage format that stores node metadata columnar-style and packs node payloads into shared value arrays recoverable from the metadata. Benchmark the baseline and alternative formats against each other for encoded size plus encode/decode performance on representative history snapshots, and capture the tradeoffs and recommendation.","acceptance_criteria":"1) An alternative HistorySnapshot representation is prototyped for benchmarking, using columnar node metadata plus shared value storage. 2) Benchmarks compare the baseline and alternative snapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative datasets. 3) Results are recorded with a clear comparison and recommendation on whether to keep, discard, or further iterate on the columnar format.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T07:09:28Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T07:09:28Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.1","title":"Prototype the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot wire format and codec","description":"Add the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot protobuf messages and a low-level benchmark-only codec path that stores node metadata separately from dense value arrays.","acceptance_criteria":"1) datamodel.proto contains an experimental columnar HistorySnapshot representation with node metadata and a dense PrimitiveArrayValue payload section. 2) flotsync_messages has low-level encode/decode helpers for the experimental format covering LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList. 3) The experimental codec remains isolated from the current public snapshot adapter path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:26Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:26Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.2","title":"Add correctness fixtures and roundtrip tests for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot","description":"Add representative history fixtures and correctness coverage for the experimental columnar HistorySnapshot codec, including boundary nodes, tombstones, null payloads, and empty arrays where allowed by schema.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic fixtures cover LatestValueWins, LinearString, and LinearList history streams with representative edge cases. 2) Experimental-format roundtrip tests verify node equivalence and reconstructed CRDT integrity. 3) Edge cases cover boundary nodes, tombstones, nullable values, and empty array payloads where valid.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:31Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:31Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-vrt.6.3","title":"Benchmark node-oriented vs experimental columnar HistorySnapshot formats and record a recommendation","description":"Add a benchmark harness comparing the current node-oriented HistorySnapshot format with the experimental columnar alternative for encoded size, encode/decode cost, and reconstruction cost, then record the results and recommendation.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Benchmarks compare current and experimental HistorySnapshot formats for encoded size and encode/decode performance on representative fixtures. 2) The benchmark harness includes medium and large histories plus tombstone-heavy cases. 3) Results are written down with a clear keep/discard/iterate recommendation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:37Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-03T13:51:37Z"}

Include nullable decode support and typed error reporting for mismatches.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:21:39Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:21:39Z"}

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-nad","title":"Adjust tombstone row semantics and indexed-id cleanup","description":"Update InMemoryData row semantics so tombstones still count in len/is_empty and are included in snapshots, return tombstoned rows from get_row, allow applying remote updates to deleted rows while still rejecting local modify_row on deleted rows, remove redundant total-order helper constructors, move uuid to workspace deps, clean up flotsync_messages indexed-id aliases/imports, and make proto chunk_index fields required.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T17:23:21Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-04T17:23:21Z"}

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-26h","title":"Draft communication protocol spec for sync/migration","description":"Create an implementation-oriented high-level protocol spec document for FlotSync covering session model, summary/range/update/ack semantics, replay/recovery, and replication group migration (including schema change via migration).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T17:15:39Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-07T17:15:39Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-b5v","title":"Revise protocol spec after review round","description":"Apply review feedback to communication_protocol_spec.md: global causality with read VV, directional state model clarity, Update vs UpdateBatch split, Ack promise semantics, migration-focused 6.6, remove NeedSnapshot and reset/recovery framing, and reduce session-oriented requirements.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T17:51:16Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-07T17:51:16Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-4s9","title":"Restructure protocol spec into 3 sub-protocols","description":"Refactor communication protocol spec to separate PeerDiscovery+Tracking, GroupBroadcast, and Replication with distinct state models, message sets, and cross-protocol flows.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T16:04:09Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-08T16:04:09Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-yhe","title":"Add discovery identity-auth details and simplify broadcast active states","description":"Update communication protocol spec to include key-based identity verification for Hello/CheckIn/StillHere and simplify GroupBroadcast active state model by demoting terminal outcomes to bookkeeping.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T16:31:27Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-08T16:31:27Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-1a9","title":"Refactor broadcast envelope durability and relay indexing","description":"Unify BroadcastEnvelope/RelayStore into one GroupMessageEnvelope with cleartext message_ref and delivery_class, remove RelayFetch, and align flows with NeedRange as best-effort broadcast request.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-08T17:38:43Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-08T17:38:43Z"}

Evaluate crate placement (flotsync_messages vs flotsync_core) and specify how database-level operations compose with schema-level operations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T14:29:54Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-05T14:29:54Z"}

{"id":"flotsync-ca7","title":"Create flotsync_replication crate and define boundaries","description":"Introduce a dedicated flotsync_replication crate as the home for replication-group and protocol orchestration APIs above schema-level operations. Define crate responsibilities and boundaries vs flotsync_core (VV/identifiers) and flotsync_messages (wire codecs). Deliverable is an architecture note and crate boundary contract, no implementation required in this task.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-10T08:32:05Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-10T08:32:05Z"}

Completed on codex/flotsync-pzi: finalized flotsync_replication API sketch updates (module split, boxed-future utility reuse, batched provider contracts, IDs/errors submodules, and one-shot invitation responder).

Scope: single mio driver thread and listener-owned accepts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z"}

new_value: Completed: added flotsync_io workspace crate skeleton with api/driver/kompact/errors modules, moved kompact to workspace dependencies, and verified with cargo check -p flotsync_io -p flotsync_discovery.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-qzw","title":"Implement single-thread mio driver core","description":"Implement poll loop, token registry, wakeup path, socket table management, and command/event control flow for one driver thread.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z"}

new_value: Completed: implemented a dedicated mio driver thread with crossbeam control-plane channel, waker-based wakeup path, handle and token registries, async command dispatch plumbing, and unit coverage for startup/shutdown, wakeups, FIFO draining, and slot reuse.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-s86","title":"Implement shared ingress and egress buffer pools","description":"Add reusable buffer pool layer with drop-based lease return semantics for both read and write paths, plus suspension/resume hooks when capacity is exhausted or restored.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z"}

new_value: Completed: added shared ingress and egress pool handles with validated driver-owned config, async FIFO egress reservations, try-based ingress buffers with async capacity waiters, a drop-notified IoLease abstraction for lease-backed payloads, and unit coverage for fairness, exhaustion, wakeups, and reuse.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-xko","title":"Implement UDP core: bind/send/recv for connected and unconnected sockets","description":"Support core UDP operations and event flow for raw bytes on the new driver.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z"}

new_value: Completed: implemented real mio-backed UDP bind/connect/send/recv/close handling with driver-owned socket state, event emission for success and failure paths, ingress-capacity read suspend/resume, reusable scratch-buffer linearisation for lease-backed datagrams, and UDP integration tests for unconnected, connected, invalid-target, and suspend/resume behavior.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-743","title":"Refactor flotsync_io driver module layout","description":"Split flotsync_io/src/driver.rs into smaller transport- and concern-specific modules before additional UDP/TCP work, keeping behavior unchanged. This was discovered while reviewing and extending flotsync-xko.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T17:35:12Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-16T17:35:12Z"}

new_value: Completed: split flotsync_io driver into focused registry, runtime, thread, tcp, and udp modules, moved UDP-specific tests beside the transport implementation, and kept behavior unchanged.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-ztx","title":"Implement TCP stream client path","description":"Support connect, raw byte send/recv, and close variants for outbound TCP streams with driver-managed lifecycle events.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:18Z"}

new_value: Completed: implemented outbound TCP stream client support with nonblocking connect completion, partial-write send progression, ingress-pool-based read suspend/resume, explicit connect failure reporting, graceful close-after-flush, abort close, and loopback coverage for connect/send/recv, failure, backpressure, graceful close, and ingress exhaustion.

old_value: {"id":"flotsync-hn4","title":"Implement Kompact IoDriver and IoBridge typed integration","description":"Expose the API via Kompact-native typed ports/channels with shared driver state and lightweight per-client bridges.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"bathtor@googlemail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:19Z","created_by":"Lars Kroll","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:55:19Z"}
